macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 on Pace for New Record High Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 is set to hit a fresh all-time high at market close, despite weeks of uncertainty from the war with Iran.[1]

items.title: US Economy Resilient with 4.3% Unemployment and Near 2% GDP Growth
items.summary: Despite shocks like fiscal spending from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, Middle East energy disruptions, and lingering trade war inflation, US unemployment remains at 4.3% and GDP growth hovers just below 2%.[2]

items.title: Historic Energy Supply Shock from Middle East Conflict Drives Oil Spike
items.summary: The ongoing war has triggered the largest energy supply shock in history, with oil prices surging and US officials preparing for potential $200 per barrel levels; the Strait's reopening or global economic slowdown are key resolutions.[4]

items.title: Fed Waller Speech Scheduled Amid Upcoming Economic Data Releases
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Waller is set to speak at 6:30 PM UTC, alongside key releases like US API Crude Oil Stock Change, Michigan Consumer Sentiment, and inflation expectations.[3]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $78K to 11-Week High on Trump Ceasefire Extension
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed above $78,000, up 2-4.3% in 24 hours, driven by President Trump's indefinite US ceasefire extension with Iran, boosting risk-on sentiment. Ethereum rose 3%, with spot Bitcoin ETFs seeing sixth straight day of inflows and strong institutional demand.

items.title: Crypto Market Rallies with Bitcoin at $78,794 and Altcoin Gains
items.summary: Bitcoin hit $78,794 (up 4.3%), XRP at $1.44 (up 1.6%), and Solana at $87.37 (up 2.7%) amid ETF inflows, though WLFI token plummeted due to legal issues. Analysts note ending post-rally recovery trends with DeFi stress.

items.title: Tron Founder Justin Sun Sues Trump-Linked World Liberty Financial
items.summary: Justin Sun filed a federal lawsuit in California against the Trump family's World Liberty Financial, alleging they blacklisted his wallet after his $75M investment, stripped voting rights, and threatened to destroy his holdings.

items.title: Arbitrum Security Council Freezes $71M ETH from Kelp Exploit
items.summary: Arbitrum froze 30,766 ETH (worth $71M) linked to the Kelp exploit, recovering about a quarter of stolen funds via a governance-controlled wallet. Circle faces class action suit over not freezing hack-related stablecoins.

items.title: $7.9B Bitcoin Options Expiry Looms at Key $75K Level
items.summary: The crypto market braces for high volatility from $7.9B Bitcoin options expiry, with $75K acting as a critical psychological and technical level influencing trading behavior.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT Image 2.5 with Massive Leap in Photorealism
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT Image 2.5 achieves the biggest jump in AI image generation in over a year, featuring perfect photorealistic human faces, advanced lighting, accurate text in images, and flawless screenshots. It surged over 300 Elo on LLM Arena, enabling production-grade ads and editable photos from text prompts.

items.title: SpaceX Partners with Cursor on New AI Coding Models for $10 Billion Deal
items.summary: SpaceX announced a partnership with AI coding startup Cursor, providing compute resources while Cursor contributes developer market share and coding model expertise. The deal includes $10 billion payment with an option to acquire Cursor for $60 billion.

items.title: Anthropic Faces Compute Shortages Amid Surging Revenue and Model Demands
items.summary: Anthropic surpasses OpenAI in annual recurring revenue but struggles with insufficient compute to serve current models or develop next-generation ones. Other labs like OpenAI are cutting projects to focus resources.

items.title: Espressif and Bosch Launch Magnetic Interaction Solution for AI Toys and Hardware
items.summary: Espressif partners with Bosch Sensortec on a low-cost magnetic sensor solution using ESP32 SoCs for real-time accessory recognition, position tracking, and motion detection in AI toys and smart hardware.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced with PS5 Demo Available Now
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 during Sony’s June State of Play, allowing soulslike fans to play a demo immediately on PS5.[2]

items.title: Bloodstained Sequel 'The Scarlet Engagement' Revealed at State of Play
items.summary: ArtPlay unveiled The Scarlet Engagement, a new Bloodstained game differing from Ritual of the Night, during the June PlayStation State of Play.[2]

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles Set for September Launch
items.summary: A remaster of Final Fantasy Tactics titled The Ivalice Chronicles is launching in September, revealed at June's PlayStation State of Play.[1]

items.title: Silent Hill f Release Date Advanced, Coming Sooner Than Expected
items.summary: Silent Hill f has a release date sooner than anticipated, announced during the June 2025 PlayStation State of Play.[1]

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Ranks Among Top 5 Best-Selling in US Series History After 2 Months
items.summary: Just two months after launch, Resident Evil Requiem is one of the top five best-selling Resident Evil games in US dollar sales, trailing only RE5, RE4 (2023), Village, and original RE4.[3]

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft revealed the full list of games joining Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.[2]

items.title: No More Heroes Developer Announces New Ultraviolent Sci-Fi Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture revealed a new over-the-top action game blending No More Heroes and Dead Rising vibes.[2]

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 announced the upcoming Force of Law Warbond for the cooperative shooter.[2]

items.title: Xbox Schedules Summer Showcase and Gears of War: E-Day Direct for June 7
items.summary: Xbox announced its Summer Showcase and a dedicated Gears of War: E-Day Direct for June 7.[6]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: TSMC Delays Adoption of ASML's High-NA EUV Machines Until After 2029
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. will postpone using ASML's advanced high-NA EUV lithography machines, costing over €350M each, for chip production through 2029 to cut costs.

items.title: GitHub Enables Default Client-Side Telemetry Collection from CLI Users
items.summary: GitHub has started collecting pseudonymous telemetry data from command-line interface users, enabling it by default.

items.title: Tesla Plans to Use Intel's 14A Process for Terafab Chip Project
items.summary: Elon Musk announced Tesla will leverage Intel's 14A process technology for chips at its Terafab project, positioning Tesla as the first major customer.

items.title: Sabre Completes $1.1 Billion Sale of Hospitality Software to TPG
items.summary: Sabre has finalized the $1.1 billion sale of its hospitality software business to private equity firm TPG.

items.title: FAA Approves United Airlines' Starlink In-Flight Service
items.summary: The FAA has approved United Airlines' implementation of Starlink satellite internet service on flights.
